title: kochia scoparia subsp. culta
intro: Kochia scoparia subsp. culta, also known as burning bush, is a plant species in the amaranth family that is native to East Asia and has been naturalized in North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of kochia scoparia subsp. culta include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antiviral agent, making it a potential treatment for various diseases. Herbal preparations of kochia scoparia subsp. culta include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
